content:
I've panicked or deadlocked my Linux USB stack just by rebooting/crashing a connected M1 machine at the wrong time at least a dozen times. And I wasn't even trying to fuzz anything.

Rust better get into Linux fast. And then the whole USB stack needs to be rewritten in it.

from https://twitter.com/marcan42/status/1534610219598495745

I've panicked or deadlocked my Linux USB stack just by rebooting/crashing a connected M1 machine at the wrong time at least a dozen times. And I wasn't even trying to fuzz anything. Rust better get into Linux fast. And then the whole USB stack needs to be rewritten in it.

- Hector Martin (@marcan42)

Is marcan42 already here? I remember him asking about this Zulip.

Yerkebulan Tulibergenov said:

I've panicked or deadlocked my Linux USB stack just by rebooting/crashing a connected M1 machine at the wrong time at least a dozen times. And I wasn't even trying to fuzz anything.

Rust better get into Linux fast. And then the whole USB stack needs to be rewritten in it.

I don't think Rust will prevent you from deadlocking :shrug: But a Rust USB stack would be awesome still :)
The deadlocking here is because a kernel thread crashed I believe. Rust would reduce the likelyhood that the kernel thread crashes in the first place.

Is marcan42 already here? I remember him asking about this Zulip.

IIRC @Finn Behrens replied to him back then in Twitter, but I haven't got a request to join yet.
He said invite only is a turn off. So maybe he lost interest over that
